text: Large language models are advanced AI systems that learn from huge amounts of text data. They work by predicting the next word in a sentence, using patterns from their training data. This data includes books, websites, and articles, which helps the model understand language. Users interact with LLMs through prompt engineering—writing clear instructions or questions to get useful responses. These models have impressive capabilities, such as writing essays, answering questions, and even translating languages. However, they also have limits. They may produce incorrect or biased information, and they lack true understanding or common sense. LLMs simply mimic patterns, so they are tools that require careful use.
